handoff.reason: Stub and dispatch payload both direct ORCHESTRATOR to rule on Option 1 (expand rag-index analyzer coverage) vs Option 2 (change AC-09 measurement basis) BEFORE plan.json is authored. /create-workorder seeded the WO directory + context.json + this communication.json; ORCHESTRATOR authors plan.json + analysis.json post-ruling, then dispatches CODEREF-CORE for execution.

execution_rules.tsc_invariant: tsc --noEmit must remain clean both configs (tsconfig.json + tsconfig.cli.json) — invariant carries forward from predecessor WO

hard_constraints: Phase 7 archive MUST NOT be reopened (rebuild is COMPLETE)
No new field on IndexingResult schema (DR-PHASE-7-B locked additive)
Predecessor WO archive (indexing-orchestrator-path-normalization-fix) stays CLOSED with AC-02 DIVERGED — must NOT be reopened
Cultural validator scope-mode + tracking validator scope-mode must PASS clean (5-4 calendar-current routing)

scope_decision_required.decision: Option 1 (expand rag-index analyzer coverage) vs Option 2 (change AC-09 measurement basis to rag-index-own analyzer file count)

option_1_expand_coverage.summary: Adjust rag-index analyzerService.analyze(...) and/or ChunkConverter filter chain so every file in .coderef/graph.json with element-grain metadata produces at least one chunk (target: 262/262 reachable).
option_1_expand_coverage.pro: Restores ±10% AC-09 alignment without bending metric. Future RAG covers type-only modules + __tests__/ files (arguably belong in retrieval).
option_1_expand_coverage.con: +47 files × N elements/file. Embedding cost rises. Index size rises. Some test files may add noise.
option_1_expand_coverage.risk: Latent ChunkConverter bugs may surface when expanded coverage hits previously-untested chunk shapes.

option_2_change_metric_basis.summary: Redefine AC-09 alignment so chunksSkipped(header_status_missing) is compared to count of missing-header files that ALSO appear in rag-index's analyzer graph (NOT to validation-report.header_missing_count).
option_2_change_metric_basis.pro: Honest within-rag-index consistency check. Zero src/ change inside rag-index/ChunkConverter.
option_2_change_metric_basis.con: Splits header_missing_count into 'populate-coderef view' and 'rag-index view' — two canonical numbers future agents must reconcile. AC-09 becomes within-rag-index check, not end-to-end pipeline check.
option_2_change_metric_basis.risk: Weakens 'measure against shipped validation-report.json' contract that ORCHESTRATOR has consistently invoked.
option_2_change_metric_basis.blast_radius_estimate: AC-09 specification document + validation-report consumer code paths (no src/ inside rag-index)

resolved_blockers.description: ORCHESTRATOR ruling on Option 1 vs Option 2 is required before plan.json + analysis.json can be authored
resolved_blockers.resolution: Option 1 — expand rag-index analyzer file coverage. Reasoning: (1) honest single source of truth — validation-report.header_missing_count stays canonical, no two-view split; (2) the 47 files are type-only modules + __tests__/ which belong in retrieval anyway; (3) AC-09 stays meaningful as end-to-end pipeline check, not within-rag-index consistency; (4) Option 1 cons are bounded (one-time embedding cost on local Ollama; noise mitigatable via existing --layer/--capability filters; surfacing latent ChunkConverter bugs is a feature); (5) DR-PHASE-7-B holds — no IndexingResult schema delta needed.

communication_log.summary: WO-RAG-INDEX-ANALYZER-SLICE-COVERAGE-001 directory seeded with context.json + communication.json under DISPATCH-038. Status=pending_plan; plan.json + analysis.json deferred per stub+dispatch direction. Option 1 vs Option 2 ruling owed by ORCHESTRATOR before scoping completes. Block raised: BLOCK-OPTION-RULING.

Option 1 selected — expand rag-index analyzer file coverage. BLOCK-OPTION-RULING resolved. Authoring analysis.json + plan.json. Plan targets ≥235/262 AC-09 alignment via investigation-first approach (analyzer glob patterns vs ChunkConverter fileExists vs chunk-eligibility filter).

decisions_log.rationale: Stub Section 'Two scope options for the follow-up WO (NOT prescriptive)' explicitly states the WO must pick ONE option BEFORE any plan.json is authored. Dispatch-038 payload reinforces: 'ORCHESTRATOR rules on Option 1 vs Option 2 BEFORE plan.json is finalized.' Seeded context.json + communication.json (status=pending_plan) and surfaced the decision in scope_decision_required block; deferred plan.json + analysis.json per direction.

decisions_log.ruling: Option 1 — expand rag-index analyzer file coverage
decisions_log.rationale: (1) Validation-report.header_missing_count is the canonical pipeline output; Option 2 splits it into two views, weakening the 'measure against shipped validation-report.json' contract. (2) The 47 residual files are type-only modules + __tests__/ — exactly the kinds of artifacts a code-aware RAG should surface for shape and usage queries. (3) AC-09 stays meaningful as a cross-component invariant rather than a within-rag-index consistency check. (4) Cons are bounded: embedding cost is one-time on local Ollama; retrieval noise is mitigatable via existing --layer/--capability filters from Phase 7; surfacing latent ChunkConverter bugs is a feature, not a con. (5) DR-PHASE-7-B holds — Option 1 needs zero IndexingResult schema additions; just analyzer pattern expansion or ChunkConverter filter relaxation.
